author: @rayg436
text: I thought you were going to put a Bluetooth circuit in there. But an aux cable works too. Good job Luke.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
See I should have added a Bluetooth module I reckon, The only reason I didn't do it, is that I built it from stuff we had laying around. The aux works surprisingly well, I have a USB-c adapter and a lighting adapter depending on who wants to use it lol 😂.
Thanks mate! :)

author: @paulwright5440
text: What did you use to cut the acrylic

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I used a band saw to cut the acrylic, although a hacksaw would do the trick! Hope this helps mate. :)

author: @michaelcorbidge7914
text: Can the batteries power a small drone ?

author: @deslomeslager
text:
Uh, wireless? Without a wire there is no sound. Your title is misleading mate. As a speaker, it is O Kay. But I would not do without real stereo sound, and if I use 2 speakers, one would need to be real for bass/mid and the other for mid/high. Your smaller speaker is just a TV speaker (I guess) so for all tones.
Why are you opting for batteries in series? I would definitely go for parallel (no BMS needed).
So there is a lot you can and should do better.
And yes, this is all ment as positive feedback! We all get better from it.

author: @garethb5729
text: Awesome job Luke. Nice one. 👍

author: @edwardhughes352
text: Where was the wireless receiver?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I didn't use a receiver, I used aux as my sound input, that way i didn't have to get a Bluetooth module, nor sacrifice battery life in the process. 👍 :)

author: @michaelsams6873
text: Really Cool!!!

author: @ColinWatters
text: If you fancy playing with Digital Signal Processing there is a company called TinySine that makes a 2×50W +100W Amplifier PCB with Bluetooth that has a DSP engine in it. The DSP is compatible with Sigma Studio so you can easily do things like sum the stereo inputs and low pass filter the result to generate the base signal. Delays an reverb might be possible but we haven't tried it yet. I have no connection with the company.

author: @ColinWatters
text: Great video. My son and I recently built a Bluetooth Speaker. We used a mains adaptor to power it and fitted a wireless charger module in the top so it also charges your phone while playing/streaming music.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
That is awesome! I should have put a Bluetooth module in there but I didn't want to spend any money on it LOL! I'm a bit of a cheapskate 😂.
Thank you mate! :)

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: Very cool. Quality sounds great. Only suggestions I can think of at the moment are possibly adding some signal filtering for each respective speaker and rubber feet to keep it from sliding from vibrations.

replies.author: @Teknopottu
replies.text: Signal filtering? Are you talking about adding components for high and low pass?
